fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Indiana CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Indiana - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity indiana, how to transfer CNA license to indiana, indiana CNA endorsement application

board.name: Indiana State Dept of Health
board.mailing_address: Indiana State Department of Health, Nurse Aide Registry, 2 North Meridian Street, Indianapolis, IN 46204

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Indiana.

steps.title: Complete an Approved Training Program
steps.description: Successfully complete an Indiana State Department of Health (ISDH) approved Nurse Aide Training Program.

steps.title: Obtain Exam Application
steps.description: Download the Indiana Nurse Aide Candidate Handbook and the Nurse Aide Competency Examination application form from the Prometric Indiana Nurse Aide Testing website.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fees to Prometric
steps.description: Complete the application form and submit it along with the required examination fees to Prometric. This can typically be done online through the Prometric portal or by mail.

steps.title: Schedule Your Examination
steps.description: Once your application is processed by Prometric, schedule your Nurse Aide Competency Examination. The exam consists of two parts: a written (or oral) portion and a skills portion.

steps.title: Pass the Competency Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass both the written (or oral) and skills portions of the Indiana Nurse Aide Competency Examination administered by Prometric.

steps.title: Be Added to the Registry
steps.description: Upon successful completion of both exam parts, Prometric will automatically add your name to the Indiana Nurse Aide Registry, certifying you as a CNA in Indiana.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Indiana CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $65. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Indiana.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Indiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Indiana while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in IN.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Indiana.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Indiana
hero.dek: To become a Certified Nursing Assistant in Indiana, candidates must complete an ISDH-approved training program and then register for and pass the state competency exam administered by Prometric.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Indiana
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Indiana CNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Candidates must complete an Indiana State Department of Health (ISDH) approved nurse aide training program. Then, obtain the Indiana Nurse Aide Competency Examination application from the Prometric website or your training facility. Submit the completed application and required examination fees to Prometric online or by mail to register for the state competency exam.
